title: The Mother Rei and Psychoanalysis in NGE Split
display_title: The Mother Rei and Psychoanalysis in NGE Split
wikitext:
[]: The Mother, Rei, and Psychoanalysis in NGE [Split]             Quote Post by SJCrew » Sun Jun 27, 2010 2:21 am [Split from here .] Rei is Rei, not yui, therefore Shinji is not banging his mother

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Sun Jun 27, 2010 2:31 am I agree with you that a romantic relationship with Rei wouldn't be incestuous.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jun 27, 2010 2:38 am Rei is associated heavily with Yui, and with the mother of all humanity besides. NGE is also full of psychoanalytical content, which is big on the whole "wanting to sleep with your mommy or daddy" thing. Ultimately, however, it seems way, way beside the point to debate whether Shinji being with Rei is "incestuous" or not. The fact is that he SHOULDN'T be with her, because Rei represents something that he's meant to break away from, not continue to bury his sense of self and independence within.

[]:             Quote Post by SJCrew » Sun Jun 27, 2010 3:05 am The fact is that he SHOULDN'T be with her, because Rei represents something that he's meant to break away from, not continue to bury his sense of self and independence within.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jun 27, 2010 3:37 am SJCrew wrote: plz explain

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jun 27, 2010 4:09 am Reichu wrote: From a thematic perspective, the concept of Rei and Shinji as a couple goes against one of the show's messages

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jun 27, 2010 4:26 am @NemZ: You seem to be splitting hairs a bit. Rei is established pretty concretely as a mother figure, and NGE is pretty concrete in terms of the mother stuff I just outlined. Shinji and Rei's relationship goes beyond just these particular broad thematic strokes, but that doesn't mean the things that I mentioned are not there. NGE has many layers. Not sure what's wrong with my comment about "This feels wrong". Rei and Shinji merged together is basically a symbol of "everything is one". Shinji merged in a coital position with the Great Mother is the visual symbol that represents the existence that Shinji rejects, and he says it's wrong. While he is not necessarily saying "bumping bottoms with Rei is wrong", the very fact that NGE only has Shinji unite with Rei under these circumstances is what I forward as thematically relevant.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sun Jun 27, 2010 4:39 am NemZ wrote: I'm going to have to disagree with you on that one because if anything their relationship has largely been the other way around, with Shinji being the one who dotes on Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by SJCrew » Sun Jun 27, 2010 5:23 am I'm not quite sure what you're asking for, so if you don't get what you want, I take no responsibility. NGE acts in part as a coming-of-age story, and it uses a lot of psychoanalytical motifs, especially maternal ones. For example, the Evangelions, which are huge mother symbols upon which the young adults depend for identity, worth, etc. In growing up, children have to break away from their mothers and find their own place in the world, one that is not dependent. Entering an Eva to pilot is correlated to returning to the womb. And, as it goes, during HIP, all of humanity returns to the primordial womb of the Chamber of Guf. It is here that we see Shinji and Rei in union, but it is not something the film celebrates. These two have only been brought together like this through the ultimate infantilization of humanity. And Shinji is not enjoying himself and notes how wrong the situation is. From a thematic perspective, the concept of Rei and Shinji as a couple goes against one of the show's messages, being roughly: Don't run back to mommy and/or all that she represents. Break free of the First Other and find the person you're meant to be. Of course, you can replace "mommy" with whatever other thing you find yourself hopelessly dependent upon, or some situation that is impeding your development as a person. "Mother" is a terribly useful archetype for getting this idea across (among others). Needless to say, Rei is NGE's biggest Mother Symbol. Sticks-out-of-the-atmosphere big. And returning at last to the "incest" thing... In-show, Rei is "forbidden" because of what she represents within the show. I get the feeling that it is common to argue about incest because somewhere along the line, Rei is mentally partitioned from her thematic functions, hence folks grasp at "concrete" reasons for her to be forbidden: she's Shinji's mom so they'll have six-fingered babies, she's part Angel so it's Rishathra, etc. And cue the circular shipper/anti-shipper debates...

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sun Jun 27, 2010 9:41 am SJCrew wrote: Rei's direct order and role in the battle, as given by Misato, was to protect Unit-01.

[]:             Quote Post by sephirotic » Sun Jun 27, 2010 9:53 am Reichu wrote: @NemZ: You seem to be splitting hairs a bit. Rei is established pretty concretely as a mother figure, and NGE is pretty concrete in terms of the mother stuff I just outlined. Shinji and Rei's relationship goes beyond just these particular broad thematic strokes, but that doesn't mean the things that I mentioned are not there. NGE has many layers. Not sure what's wrong with my comment about "This feels wrong". Rei and Shinji merged together is basically a symbol of "everything is one". Shinji merged in a coital position with the Great Mother is the visual symbol that represents the existence that Shinji rejects, and he says it's wrong. While he is not necessarily saying "bumping bottoms with Rei is wrong", the very fact that NGE only has Shinji unite with Rei under these circumstances is what I forward as thematically relevant.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jun 27, 2010 1:19 pm SJCrew wrote: Thing is, Rei isn't actually mother figure to Shinji, and Shinji doesn't depend on her at all.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Sun Jun 27, 2010 1:41 pm NemZ wrote: But she doesn't guide him at all in the EoE ending; she just passively accepts his directions even when she indicates it probably isn't a good idea.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jun 27, 2010 1:58 pm Lucretius wrote: I don't know about that; I see her acting as Shinji's psychopomp in EoE. She tries to guide him in a certain direction, ultimately leading him to reject Instrumentality and get over his shit.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Sun Jun 27, 2010 2:05 pm NemZ wrote: Exactly my point. Yes Rei certainly has many strong connections to mother figures, but she herself does not fill that role. If anything she's more of an anti-mother both in EoE and 23, not giving life but instead taking others with her into death.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jun 27, 2010 3:07 pm The tangent has its own thread now. I'm a bit surprised that some find the thematic idea "Rei = Mother" so unpalatable (or insert adjective of choice). Given NGE's heavy psychoanalytic (there's that word again) influences, it seemed like one of those "givens" to me, but I guess this is going to be harder than I thought! My impression right now is that Rei's "Mother" aspect is being confused with another of Rei's aspects, as one of the youths who is coming of age. Rei is, too, one of the "teenagers" in the process of growing up and breaking free of what her identity has relied upon. Story-wise, those things have been piloting Eva and, obviously, Gendo. The Eva gets blown sky-high, of course. And when Rei decides to reject Gendo, he's provided her a nice symbolic umbilical cord that she can cut (his arm inserted directly into her navel). Her relationship with Shinji is, largely, more related to Rei's "coming of age story" than her Mother Symbol status. But, as it goes, Rei "growing up" involves accepting and returning to her true self, which is... well, guess. Additionally, there seems to be the expectation that Shinji acknowledge Rei as a mother figure on a fully conscious level, and for Rei to consistently fall under the protective/nourishing side of the Mother archetype, in order for the psychoanalytic framework to be present and operable. Lucretius' post is right on the money. Also, sephirotic's response to the "incest" issue was well put, IMO (English problems aside ).

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Jun 27, 2010 3:50 pm My own two cents are pretty much in line with Reichu's and Lucretius' --which are better worded than I could ever put it. (I think SaltyJoe is on the right track with things, too.) And for what it's worth, here was my readings on the relevant episode 6 lines: Episode 6 wrote: Rei: Why do you say that? You won't die. Because I'll protect you.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jun 27, 2010 7:17 pm Reichu wrote: I'm a bit surprised that some find the thematic idea "Rei = Mother" so unpalatable (or insert adjective of choice).

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Jun 27, 2010 7:35 pm NemZ : Perhaps, like so many things in NGE, the quotes in question as well as Rei's own status (infertile, bringer of death instead of giver of life) can go both ways? Just a thought.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Jun 27, 2010 8:19 pm I'm amazed nobody's brought up the fact that Rei, despite not having Yui's soul, is genetically Yui with some Lilith mixed in . The main problem with incest is the genetic problems that occur in the offspring of two people as closely related as a mother and a son or a brother and a sister. This is why it's considered "OK" that so much incest occurred in Genesis: these genetic problems would not arise that early in the gene pool. We don't know how much of the Rei genome is from Yui and how much is from Lilith, but considering she exists due to a failed attempt to salvage Yui and she looks way more like Yui than Lilith, I'm guessing she's mostly Yui. Thus a Shinji/Rei pairing would result in the same problems as incest. It's incest even disregarding that; Lilith is Shinji's ancestor. His first ancestor. If Shinji were to have sex with Rei, he would have sex with someone who is partially his mother and partially his great^X grandmother. And yes, Rei's soul is that of Lilith. It's not necessarily Lilith's entire soul, but the soul is entirely Lilith's.
